page.title: SSL/TLS Certificate Management
page.description: Manage ACME certificates with wildcard, DNS validation, multi-CA and auto-deploy support

masterCert.detected: Certificate for master domain {{domain}} has been issued
masterCert.deployDesc: After deployment, Nginx will be auto-installed with SSL configured and HTTPS enabled
masterCert.deploySuccess: Master certificate deployed, redirecting to HTTPS

https.enableDesc: This will install Nginx and set up a reverse proxy for MiaomiaowuX, enabling HTTPS access for domain {{domain}}. Continue?

certTable.title: Certificate List
certTable.empty: No certificates yet, click "Apply Certificate" to start

createDialog.title: Apply SSL/TLS Certificate
createDialog.description: Supports wildcard certificates (*.example.com), DNS validation and multi-CA
createDialog.dnsProviderHint: Please add DNS API credentials in the "DNS Providers" tab first
createDialog.autoDeployDesc: Auto-deploy and reload service after renewal or new server installation

dnsProviderTable.title: DNS Providers
dnsProviderTable.description: Manage DNS API credentials for DNS-01 validation of wildcard certificates
dnsProviderTable.empty: No DNS providers yet, please add one to support wildcard certificate applications

dnsProviderDialog.title: Add DNS Provider
dnsProviderDialog.description: Configure DNS API credentials for DNS-01 validation
dnsProviderDialog.name: Name

deleteDialog.title: Confirm Delete Certificate
deleteDialog.description: Are you sure you want to delete the certificate for domain {{domain}}? This action cannot be undone.

deployDialog.title: Deploy Certificate
deployDialog.description: Deploy certificate {{domain}} to master server and all remote servers

uploadDialog.title: Upload Certificate
uploadDialog.description: Manually upload SSL/TLS certificate, paste PEM format certificate and private key content
uploadDialog.apiUploadDesc: You can also upload certificates via API Token: POST /api/admin/certificates/upload, add Authorization: Bearer <API Token> header, Body is JSON: {"domain": "example.com", "cert_pem": "<base64>", "key_pem": "<base64>"}. API Token can be obtained in system settings.

toast.certDeployed: Certificate deployed to master server and all remote servers
toast.fillDomainEmail: Please fill in domain and email
toast.dnsProviderRequired: DNS validation mode requires selecting a DNS provider
toast.fillNameCredentials: Please fill in name and credentials
toast.invalidJson: Invalid credentials format, please enter valid JSON
toast.fillDomainCertKey: Please fill in domain, certificate and private key
